Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Moss is a pixel canvas where every brush is a tiny program 209 upvotes, 25 comments
  2. Flip Distance of Convex Triangulations and Tree Rotation Is NP-Complete 21 upvotes, 0 comments
  3. NanoGPT Slowrun: Language Modeling with Limited Data, Infinite Compute 136 upvotes, 26 comments
  4. Qwen3.5 Fine-Tuning Guide 301 upvotes, 70 comments
  5. Building a new Flash 409 upvotes, 115 comments
  6. Libre Solar – Open Hardware for Renewable Energy 227 upvotes, 63 comments
  7. Jensen Huang says Nvidia is pulling back from OpenAI and Anthropic 45 upvotes, 8 comments
  8. MyFirst Kids Watch Hacked. Access to Camera and Microphone 123 upvotes, 33 comments
  9. “It turns out” (2010) 259 upvotes, 83 comments
  10. An interactive map of Flock Cams 542 upvotes, 198 comments
  11. The View from RSS 91 upvotes, 26 comments
  12. Something is afoot in the land of Qwen 578 upvotes, 259 comments
  13. Daemon (2006) 32 upvotes, 8 comments
  14. Google Workspace CLI 250 upvotes, 107 comments
  15. Raspberry Pi Pico as AM Radio Transmitter 82 upvotes, 31 comments
  16. Dario Amodei calls OpenAI’s messaging around military deal ‘straight up lies’ 358 upvotes, 173 comments
  17. A bit of fluid mechanics from scratch not from scratch 39 upvotes, 13 comments
  18. Malm Whale 14 upvotes, 3 comments
  19. Humans 40k yrs ago developed a system of conventional signs 81 upvotes, 39 comments
  20. NRC issues first commercial reactor construction approval in 10 years [pdf] 67 upvotes, 33 comments
  21. Chaos and Dystopian news for the dead internet survivors 53 upvotes, 25 comments
  22. Glaze by Raycast 205 upvotes, 125 comments
  23. Picking Up a Zillion Pieces of Litter 59 upvotes, 29 comments
  24. Was Windows 1.0's lack of overlapping windows a legal or a technical matter? 65 upvotes, 41 comments
  25. BMW Group to deploy humanoid robots in production in Germany for the first time 97 upvotes, 79 comments
  26. MacBook Neo 1650 upvotes, 1950 comments
  27. Show HN: A GFM+GF-MathJax/Latex HTML formatting adventure 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  28. US tech firms pledge at White House to bear costs of energy for datacenters 43 upvotes, 37 comments
  29. What Python's asyncio primitives get wrong about shared state 10 upvotes, 7 comments
  30. Roboflow (YC S20) Is Hiring a Security Engineer for AI Infra 0 upvotes, 0 comments
